items.title: This Nuclear Stock Is Down More Than 60% Over the Past Year and Could Be a Screaming Buy
items.summary: NuScale Power's stock has declined over 62% in the past year following a $500 million capital raise and a revenue decline from $37 million to $31.5 million. However, the article argues the company remains well-positioned as the only small modular reactor developer with U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission design approvals, making it attractive to data center operators seeking solutions for AI computing power demands.

items.title: Prediction: Here's What a $5,000 Investment in NuScale Power (SMR) Stock Will Be Worth in 10 Years
items.summary: NuScale Power, a small modular reactor (SMR) developer, has significant growth potential driven by rising demand for nuclear energy from AI data centers. The company is the first to receive NRC design approval and is planning major projects including the largest U.S. nuclear deployment program with the Tennessee Valley Authority. However, it currently posts losses, has yet to build reactors, and faces competition. A $5,000 investment could potentially multiply 10x by 2036 if the company succeeds and scales revenue, but risks include delays, cash crunches, and share dilution.

items.title: Where Will NuScale Power Stock Be in 5 Years?
items.summary: NuScale Power, a leading small modular reactor (SMR) company, is at a critical juncture with significant growth potential but substantial execution risks. While the company holds regulatory approval advantages and a major $10 trillion market opportunity, it faces mounting losses ($664M in 2025), severe shareholder dilution (1,500% share count increase since IPO), and repeated project delays. The company's first operational plant may not come online until 2030-2032, well behind original 2027 projections, leaving investors in a 'wait-and-see' mode regarding whether key catalysts will materialize.
